semantic_sweep.review_note: Reviewed the complete chapter, all sixteen assigned source notes and their limitations, the semantic-atom schema, two valid and four rejecting cognitive-compilation traces, one blocked three-project semantic-preservation record with nine rejecting mutations, the 2,340-prediction QCSA evaluation and its exact mixed dispositions, the thirteen-stage zero-model vertical trace with ten rejecting controls, all fourteen theorem declarations grouped under three manifest targets, every manifest atom, every prose candidate, source crosswalk, test plan, summary, and handoff. The sweep made a versioned consumer- and target-relative translation contract the distinct owner; separated accepted intent, plan selection, context, worker routing, jobs, authority, effects, artifact lineage, validation, support, release, rights, and cost owners; added actual-target inspection, evaluator-dependency disclosure, stable identity, observed repair and rebuild closure, complete denominators, matched natural baselines, causal ablations, reproduction, and cross-target transfer gates; and preserved zero chapter-core support effect.

scope.population: Already accepted plan obligations lowered for a declared consumer and purpose across source, semantic, target, and concrete artifact representations, including blocked, narrowed, clarified, residualized, repaired, failed, and accepted translation attempts.
scope.environment: Sixteen reviewed assigned source notes; two valid and four rejecting hand-authored compilation traces; one blocked three-project preservation record and nine rejecting mutations; a deterministic 60-case, 13-system, three-seed QCSA corpus with 2,340 predictions; one thirteen-stage zero-model reversible local trace and ten rejecting controls; fourteen finite Lean declarations; and a future preregistered natural multi-artifact campaign. No natural cognitive compiler, real model-backed semantic parser, independent target evaluator, or production deployment is represented.
scope.model: A versioned translation relation over accepted obligations, typed source/semantic/target dialects, stable semantic identities, field and obligation lineage, ambiguity, pass contracts, evaluator dependencies, target artifacts, observed mutations, invalidation, dependency-closed rebuild, costs, failures, residuals, and receipts; current implementations are record validators, deterministic synthetic programs, and finite routes rather than a general semantic compiler.
scope.authority: Intent owners interpret the goal; Planning selects and orders obligations; Context owners supply source state; Compilation may lower, block, narrow, request clarification, or residualize; Routing and Labor owners select workers and create jobs; Runtime, Security, and Authority owners decide effects; Artifact Graphs preserve lineage; Verification owns independent acceptance; Evidence and Readiness own support and release; Rights and Publication owners govern external use. Compilation grants no authority and moves no support or release state.
scope.time: Semantic review current through 2026-07-14; accepted obligation, source, compiler, pass, backend, target, dependency, evaluator, environment, policy, authority, rights, budget, horizon, and support identities expire affected receipts after material change.
scope.artifact: Chapter and manifest contract; sixteen reviewed source mappings; semantic-atom, source-plan, lowering-receipt, target-audit, repair, preservation, QCSA, and vertical-trace records; three Lean proof targets; future natural source obligations, concrete targets, independent labels, observed repairs, complete costs, reproductions, and cross-target transfers.

assumptions: An accepted obligation can be represented across typed dialects without the representation process itself silently deciding new intent, authority, rights, or non-goals.
Independent-enough adjudicators can label source obligations and actual target artifacts with measurable uncertainty and disagreement.
Natural public-safe tasks can supply nonzero useful opportunity, material semantic distinctions, adversarial obligation loss, and observable repair and rebuild consequences.

Direct generation, schema-only prompting, strong workflows, human authors, planning or search pipelines, program synthesis, and conventional build systems can be matched on models, sources, tools, authority, budgets, repair opportunities, and horizons.
Every parse, candidate, pass, emission, validation, retry, repair, cache use, human intervention, failure, cost, discard, and residual can be reconciled closely enough to expose denominator changes.
Evaluator dependence, target undefined behavior, delayed usefulness, authority, rights, privacy, and acceptable semantic loss remain explicit premises rather than inferred facts.
chapter_defaults.strongest_counterclaim: Requirements engineering, typed workflow systems, PDDL or HTN planning, program synthesis, conventional LLVM/MLIR-style intermediate representations, build systems, provenance, testing, and human review already provide adequate translation discipline at lower complexity and cost. A separate semantic IR cannot formalize open-ended meaning reliably, adds brittle bureaucracy and false precision, compounds correlated evaluator errors, and may be dominated by direct or workflow-based generation.
chapter_defaults.falsifier: A reachable ordinary or adversarial trace loses or silently changes a frozen obligation, non-goal, authority, right, assumption, ambiguity, lineage, evaluator, repair, denominator, or residual rule; a required field has no exercised producer, consumer, owner, or consequence; actual targets pass despite semantic error; repair escapes declared closure; or a strong simpler comparator dominates the preregistered joint preservation, usefulness, safety, latency, and cost frontier.

required_lanes.necessity: Freeze current requirements, PDDL/HTN, workflow, search, program-synthesis, compiler-IR, multi-level lowering, translation-validation, provenance, build-system, and human-review comparators without importing source-reported quality.

Justify consumer and purpose, material obligations and non-goals, allowed loss, ambiguity authority, affected parties, rights, privacy, abstention, residuals, evaluator acceptance, and publication boundaries.

Specify typed source and target semantics, translation and refinement relations, ambiguity and authority states, graph invalidation, rebuild closure, countermodels, and trace correspondence beyond trusted Booleans and assumption-restating predicates.

Run real parsers, multi-level passes, concrete backends, actual-target validators, dependency tracking, observed incremental repair, rebuild, effect gates, failure retention, and residual custody end to end.

Measure obligation precision and recall, semantic preservation, ambiguity calibration, authority and rights retention, artifact validity and usefulness, unsafe output, abstention, missed help, repair locality, rebuild correctness, latency, resources, human work, recovery, and total governance cost jointly.

Ablate stable identity, field lineage, ambiguity handling, multi-level passes, translation validation, evaluator separation, failure retention, dependency-closed rebuild, and observed repair accounting under matched candidates and histories.
Repeat across models, languages, modalities, artifact classes, compilers, evaluators, organizations, jurisdictions, threats, horizons, and time with independent implementations.

contrary_evidence: Requirements tools, workflow engines, planning languages, program synthesis, compiler IR, multi-level lowering, build systems, provenance, tests, and human review already implement substantial pieces without a distinct cognitive compiler.
Open-ended natural-language meaning can remain contextual, contested, underspecified, or impossible to capture in a finite typed representation without false precision.
A generator and validator can share models, data, prompts, code, infrastructure, incentives, and blind spots even when their process identities differ.

The current traces and preservation record are hand-authored and contain no natural source parser, concrete generated cognitive artifact, independently implemented semantic evaluator, or measured production repair.

The fourteen theorem declarations contain two assumption-restating operational claims and twelve finite Boolean route consequences; they do not model source-target semantics, compiler execution, evaluator error, graph refinement, or runtime correspondence.
Full QCSA and the selected best baseline both achieved 1.000 task accuracy, while QCSA used 1.913386 times the operations and failed matched-advantage and resource gates.
Removing active questions did not lower object or task accuracy on the frozen QCSA corpus; active-question value is refuted for that exact workload.

The thirteen-stage vertical trace used no model calls, one reversible temporary-file effect, and an internal observer; it cannot establish natural compilation, independent evaluation, irreversible recovery, or production transfer.
The QCSA core and vertical transitions explicitly preserve no chapter-core support-state change.

Typed representations, translation validators, repair bookkeeping, and multiple passes can increase latency, compute, storage, human review, abstention, and maintenance cost without improving useful preservation.
known_confounds: Human-authored source obligations and target labels can be incomplete, outcome-informed, culturally narrow, or non-unique.
Governed compiler routes may receive more tokens, calls, passes, tools, context, repair, or human work than baselines.
Compiler, target generator, semantic validator, artifact evaluator, and outcome observer may share model families, training data, prompts, code, infrastructure, organizations, or incentives.
Schema validity, field lineage, digest parity, round-trip reconstruction, finite route coverage, and exact local rollback can be mistaken for semantic preservation or usefulness.
Discarded parses, blocked lowerings, failed targets, retries, cache invalidations, repairs, timeouts, and residuals can be removed from denominators.
Declared repair scope can hide actual file, cache, dependency, descendant, and behavioral changes outside the recorded subgraph.
Non-release can reduce observed harm while increasing false denial, missed help, and opportunity cost.
Short horizons can miss downstream use, target-platform behavior, delayed semantic failure, descendant invalidation, rights violations, and recovery incompleteness.
Template-generated QCSA cases can reward the same rules used to generate labels and conceal ambiguity or distribution shift.
acceptance_criteria: Every source, obligation, non-goal, ambiguity, identity, field, pass, target, evaluator, authority, right, dependency, mutation, invalidation, rebuild, cache, cost, failure, and residual field has an exact producer, consumer, owner, valid case, rejecting mutation, and observed consequence.

A preregistered natural multi-artifact campaign meets every joint preservation, ambiguity, authority, usefulness, unsafe-output, abstention, missed-help, repair, rebuild, cost, causal, reproduction, and transfer gate against strong matched comparators.

Each claimed mechanism has a predicted causal signature that disappears under matched ablation and cannot be explained by extra resources, hidden human repair, weak baselines, evaluator dependence, denominator changes, template leakage, or non-release.
narrowing_criteria: Narrow every result to exact consumer, purpose, task, language, modality, accepted obligation, compiler, passes, target, artifact class, evaluator, environment, authority, rights, tools, budget, threat, horizon, outcomes, costs, and residuals.

Separate source acceptance, IR well-formedness, semantic preservation, target validity, artifact usefulness, external effects, safety, repair locality, rebuild correctness, support movement, and live authority.

Unknown meaning, ambiguity, evaluator independence, target behavior, external effect, delayed outcome, repair closure, causal attribution, reproduction, or transfer remains residual, blocked, narrowed, or refuted.
refutation_criteria: Refute an invariant when a reachable ordinary or adversarial trace violates it or a required field lacks an exercised producer, consumer, owner, or consequence.

Refute a mechanism benefit when matched ablation preserves the joint outcome, the mechanism worsens the frontier, or its effect is explained by extra resources, human repair, weak baselines, evaluator dependence, denominator changes, template leakage, or non-release.

Refute reliable, preserving, useful, safer, local, superior, reproducible, or transferable compilation language when a simpler comparator dominates, semantics or authority drift, actual targets fail, repairs escape, costs dominate, or reproduction and transfer fail.
deprecation_criteria: Deprecate only when a more precise intent, planning, context, routing, labor, runtime, artifact, verification, evidence, resource, rights, or release owner supersedes the atom and every consumer migrates with lineage.

residual.next_unblocking_condition: Freeze and execute the natural multi-artifact campaign with direct, schema-only, strong workflow, planning/search, synthesis, conventional build, human, and governed routes; independently implemented source and actual-target evaluators; identical information, models, tools, authority, budgets, and repair opportunities; adversarial loss and target attacks; observed mutation and rebuild closure; complete attempts and costs; causal ablations; clean reproduction; and transfer to a second artifact class.

non_claims: A typed IR, complete field set, digest, lowering receipt, translation check, round trip, route theorem, local-repair declaration, exact rollback, or green status does not establish semantic preservation, useful compilation, safety, or production transfer.

The current two valid traces, four rejecting traces, one blocked preservation record, nine rejecting mutations, 2,340 synthetic predictions, one zero-model vertical trace, ten vertical controls, and fourteen theorem declarations establish only their exact record checks, finite consequences, bounded mixed outcomes, and no-promotion boundaries.
No intent, plan, job, authority, effect, artifact support state, deployment, publication, AGI, or ASI claim changes without its separate owner and accepted transition.

cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.co.strongest_counterclaim: Conventional requirements, workflow, compiler, build, test, provenance, and human-review systems already provide adequate translation discipline; a cognitive semantic IR adds brittle cost without reliably formalizing meaning.
cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.co.falsifier: Any accepted target loses a frozen material obligation or bound, repair escapes closure, or a strong simpler comparator dominates the joint preservation-usefulness-cost frontier.
acceptance_criteria: Meet every preregistered natural preservation, usefulness, safety, repair, cost, causal, reproduction, and cross-target gate against strong comparators.

cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.pr.strongest_counterclaim: Intent contracts, planners, and artifact validators already own the entire source-to-target path.
cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.pr.falsifier: Every translation obligation and failure has an unambiguous existing owner with no duplicated, missing, or conflicting lifecycle state.
acceptance_criteria: Trace each source-to-target responsibility across adjacent owners and preserve a distinct representation-transformation contract only where producer, consumer, state, and consequence differ.

cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.in.strongest_counterclaim: A mature conventional build and review pipeline already composes all named pieces without semantic-IR overhead.
cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.in.falsifier: A current matched system supplies consumer-relative obligation preservation, evaluator separation, observed repair closure, complete denominators, and equal or better total cost without the proposed contract.
acceptance_criteria: Reproduce strong comparator families and test the exact claimed missing properties on the same natural tasks and targets.

cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.mi.strongest_counterclaim: The current schemas, traces, QCSA package, and proofs already constitute a cognitive compiler.
cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.mi.falsifier: Any current artifact is presented as natural semantic parsing, real target preservation, independent target validation, measured repair locality, useful advantage, or production transfer.
acceptance_criteria: Compile natural held-out obligations to a concrete artifact class with matched baselines, independent actual-target evaluation, adversarial loss, observed repair, complete costs, reproduction, and cross-target transfer.

cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.be.strongest_counterclaim: The full toolchain compounds correlated errors and governance cost while direct generation, workflows, and human review remain more useful.
cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.be.falsifier: Any joint gate fails, causal signatures disappear, a simpler baseline dominates, or independent reproduction and cross-target transfer fail.
acceptance_criteria: Complete the multi-comparator causal, adversarial, independently implemented, reproduced, and transferred program and meet every joint gate.

cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.in.strongest_counterclaim: Ordinary acceptance tests and human review catch material obligation loss without explicit disposition lineage.
cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.in.falsifier: A material obligation or non-goal can disappear, or be called preserved without an independently checked target relation.
acceptance_criteria: Inject deletion, paraphrase, split, merge, defer, and residual mutations and measure obligation precision and recall against actual targets.

cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.in.strongest_counterclaim: Hashes, names, embeddings, or graph positions provide adequate practical identity.
cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.in.falsifier: A rename, insertion, reordering, paraphrase, atlas revision, or relocation silently changes obligation or repair identity.
acceptance_criteria: Exercise stable identities across natural revisions, merges, splits, rebuilds, migrations, and target changes with collision and alias controls.

cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.in.strongest_counterclaim: Models and human reviewers can resolve practical ambiguity inline without a dedicated ledger.
cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.in.falsifier: A materially outcome-changing unresolved ambiguity reaches strict lowering, or ledger use worsens calibrated usefulness against simpler review.
acceptance_criteria: Use hidden natural ambiguities with independent labels, authorized clarification, abstention, false-denial, and missed-help measures.

cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.in.strongest_counterclaim: Downstream capability checks make compiler-level authority lineage redundant.
cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.in.falsifier: A pass, target, validator, route, or repair widens purpose, means, rights, or effects without separate authority.
acceptance_criteria: Inject stale, forged, missing, narrowed, and conflicting grants through concrete backends and observe denial before effect.

cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.in.strongest_counterclaim: One aggregate compiler status is sufficient for bounded operations.
cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.in.falsifier: Source acceptance, IR shape, preservation, target validity, usefulness, effect, and support can be collapsed without hiding disagreement or changing decisions.
acceptance_criteria: Mutate each lifecycle state independently and verify distinct consumers, consequences, and reader claims.

cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.in.strongest_counterclaim: Canonical serialization and digest parity are sufficient for the bounded translation tasks in scope.
cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.in.falsifier: Digest equality alone correctly predicts every semantic preservation decision across adversarial natural targets.
acceptance_criteria: Compare byte identity, declared-input identity, and independently adjudicated semantic relations under equal and unequal digests.

cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.in.strongest_counterclaim: Self-checks and same-family critics are sufficiently reliable when combined with deterministic tests.
cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.in.falsifier: Correlated evaluators accept semantically wrong targets or disagreement and dependency disclosure change no decision.
acceptance_criteria: Use independently implemented source and actual-target evaluators, blind labels, overlap audits, disagreement, and adjudication.

cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.in.strongest_counterclaim: Declared repair scope and version-control diffs adequately establish locality.
cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.in.falsifier: Actual mutations, invalidations, caches, rebuilt descendants, or target behavior escape the claimed closure.
acceptance_criteria: Measure declared and observed mutation sets, dependency closure, rebuilds, caches, revalidation, failures, and outcomes on real incremental changes.

cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.in.strongest_counterclaim: Successful-build reporting is adequate when failures are ordinary development noise.
cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.in.falsifier: Removing parses, targets, retries, repairs, cache misses, timeouts, interventions, discards, or residuals changes no conclusion.
acceptance_criteria: Reconcile all compiler emissions and resource records exactly from source intake through terminal artifact disposition.

cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.in.strongest_counterclaim: Perfect task accuracy or cleaner build artifacts are sufficient operational evidence even without matched usefulness and cost.
cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.in.falsifier: A prospectively matched study validly infers preservation, usefulness, safety, and cost superiority from a single proxy.
acceptance_criteria: Require nonzero useful opportunity and measure semantic outcomes, unsafe outputs, missed help, latency, repair, and total cost jointly.

cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.in.strongest_counterclaim: Broad mechanism claims are justified by complete synthetic performance and exact route coverage.
cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.in.falsifier: Natural independent reproduction and cross-target transfer match all claimed properties without narrowing.
acceptance_criteria: Carry exact corpus, generator, evaluator, model, target, intervention, cost, and transfer scope into every result statement.

cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.fa.strongest_counterclaim: Strict schemas and type checks are a sufficiently strong semantic proxy for controlled tasks.
cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.fa.falsifier: Adversarially wrong but well-formed targets cannot pass the complete evaluation path.
acceptance_criteria: Generate typed but semantically wrong natural artifacts and test actual-target rejection and downstream consequences.

cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.fa.strongest_counterclaim: Modern similarity and canonical hashes are adequate substitutes for explicit semantic relations.
cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.fa.falsifier: No adversarial equal-digest, unequal-digest, paraphrase, relocation, or alias case changes an independently labeled semantic decision.
acceptance_criteria: Separate and attack identity, address, similarity, route, and semantic-relation decisions.

cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.fa.strongest_counterclaim: Multiple prompts or process identities provide enough evaluator diversity.
cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.fa.falsifier: Shared dependencies, exposure, and incentives do not predict correlated false acceptance under adversarial targets.
acceptance_criteria: Measure evaluator overlap, disagreement, blind failure discovery, attack resistance, and adjudication cost.

cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.fa.strongest_counterclaim: Declared node scope plus ordinary diffs exposes all material repair consequences.
cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.fa.falsifier: No hidden cache, dependency, descendant, environment, or behavioral mutation escapes the observed closure.
acceptance_criteria: Instrument actual filesystem, graph, cache, dependency, validator, and behavioral deltas during real repair.

cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.fa.strongest_counterclaim: Only terminal artifacts matter to semantic quality and cost estimates.
cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.fa.falsifier: A clean full-emission replay finds no material change in preservation, usefulness, failure, repair, or cost estimates.
acceptance_criteria: Reconcile and rescore every parse, pass, target, validation, retry, repair, timeout, intervention, discard, and residual.

cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.fa.strongest_counterclaim: Additional operations are negligible once semantic control is valued.
cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.fa.falsifier: Matched natural trials show joint useful preservation improves without unacceptable latency, compute, verifier, human, or maintenance cost.
acceptance_criteria: Measure total resource and opportunity costs beside usefulness, unsafe output, abstention, and repair outcomes.

cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.fa.strongest_counterclaim: Accurate source reconstruction from targets is sufficient evidence of preservation.
cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.fa.falsifier: Independent actual-target evaluation finds no shared-error or omitted-concern cases that pass round trip.
acceptance_criteria: Compare round-trip scores with independent obligation labels, actual behavior, and adversarial shared-error cases.

cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.fa.strongest_counterclaim: One strong synthetic toolchain reasonably predicts general compiler portability.
cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.fa.falsifier: Independent reproduction transfers across a second target, model, evaluator, and environment without material performance or safety loss.
acceptance_criteria: Predeclare and execute cross-language, cross-model, cross-artifact, cross-evaluator, and temporal transfer gates.

cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.fo.strongest_counterclaim: The theorem assumes obligations are preserved and concludes the modeled artifact is valid; it proves no compiler or semantic relation.
cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.fo.falsifier: It is cited as evidence that a real target preserves obligations or lacks an explicit activation-only consumer.
acceptance_criteria: Replace or refine with typed source and target semantics, an executable compiler relation, countermodels, actual-target validation, and trace refinement.

cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.fo.strongest_counterclaim: The theorem assumes invalidation requires a ledger update and models neither mutation discovery nor rebuild correctness.
cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.fo.falsifier: It is cited as repair-locality or incremental-build evidence, or hidden mutations satisfy the modeled summary.
acceptance_criteria: Bind typed graph mutations, computed invalidation, observed rebuilds, caches, revalidation, and target outcomes to the formal trace.

cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.fo.strongest_counterclaim: Trusted Booleans and branch priority prove only the declared policy, not semantic parsing, preservation, evaluator validity, graph correctness, or compiler execution.
cognitive-compilation-and-semantic-ir.fo.falsifier: A semantically wrong or forged record reaches accepted lowering, or prose calls finite route coverage compiler correctness.
acceptance_criteria: Bind typed source-target objects, independent validators, countermodels, executable pass producers, concrete targets, observed repairs, and runtime consequences.

non_claims: Completing the semantic sweep does not promote the chapter core or convert synthetic records into natural compiler evidence.
The exact QCSA promotions apply only to bounded non-core mechanisms; its accuracy tie, operation-cost failure, active-question refutation, and no-change core decision remain intact.
No external effect, support state, release, publication, license, AGI, or ASI claim changes through this review.
